pages.full_text:
"Stand by Dame Anne"
"She was bloody marvellous"
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in coat of arms/crest with 'LUCY CAVENDISH' text (printed, not handwritten). Below the crest are two handwritten quotations: "Stand by Dame Anne" and "She was bloody marvellous". These likely refer to Dame Anne Campbell, a former Principal of Lucy Cavendish College, Cambridge. No page number visible.

pages.full_text:
Van Log  Friday 14th October 2011
                    -October 2017
86 Westfield G, Edgbaston, Birmingham B15 3Jo.

Food & drink from Ameno, Lake Orta, N. Italy
photo_captions: Food & drink from Ameno, Lake Orta, N. Italy
pages.notes: Top of page reads 'Van Log  Friday 14th October 2011 -October 2017' suggesting this is the front matter or ownership/log page for the van, covering the period October 2011 to October 2017. Address given: 86 Westfield G[?road/grove/gardens], Edgbaston, Birmingham B15 3Jo[?]. The middle initial of the street abbreviation is unclear — could be 'Rd', 'Gv', or similar. The postcode ends '3Jo' — the final character may be a letter or digit. A photo of food and drink is glued in, with a vertical caption along its left edge: 'Food & drink from Ameno, Lake Orta, N. Italy'. No handwritten page number visible.
